decks.notes: The deck focuses on using cards like Neoform and Eldritch Evolution to quickly tutor and cheat out powerful creatures like Allosaurus Rider, Griselbrand, and Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant. Planar Genesis and Generous Ent help to ramp up mana quickly, while cards like Veil of Summer and Pact of Negation protect your combo pieces. Consign to Memory can also provide card draw and utility. The deck aims to assemble its combo pieces as quickly as possible to overwhelm the opponent with big creatures and win the game.

The deck focuses on using instant reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance to bring back powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Atraxa, Grand Unifier from the graveyard at instant speed. It also utilizes cards like Solitude, Faithful Mending, and Prismatic Ending for control and removal, while also including disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan, ultimately winning the game through powerful reanimated threats and efficient control spells.

The main concept of this Esper Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's hand with cards like Inquisition of Kozilek and Thoughtseize, while also controlling the board with removal spells like Prismatic Ending and Solitude. The deck also focuses on graveyard hate with Relic of Progenitus and creature recursion with Ephemerate. Additionally, the deck utilizes card draw and filtering with Psychic Frog and Quantum Riddler, along with powerful finishers like Ketramose, the New Dawn. The land base is designed to support the three-color mana requirements of the deck efficiently.

The main focus of this deck is to generate mana quickly through spells like Manamorphose and Pyretic Ritual, while utilizing Ruby Medallion to reduce the cost of casting spells. Ral, Monsoon Mage is a key card in the combo, allowing for card draw and providing additional mana. The goal is to cast spells like Grapeshot or Past in Flames multiple times in a single turn to deal massive damage to the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Desperate Ritual and Reckless Impulse to keep the combo going and find key pieces.

This deck focuses on using aggressive creatures to quickly pressure the opponent while also utilizing blink effects to protect and reuse key cards. The deck includes powerful creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Starfield Shepherd, along with cards like Ephemerate and Ephemerate to provide protection and value. By combining aggression with blink effects, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and control the board while maintaining a strong presence on the battlefield.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures such as Emrakul, the Promised End and Drowner of Truth. It also utilizes cards like Sowing Mycospawn and Blade of the Bloodchief to generate tokens and pump up creatures for a lethal attack. The inclusion of cards like Ugin's Labyrinth and Malevolent Rumble helps control the board while also providing additional damage to opponents. Overall, the key strategy is to assemble the combo pieces quickly and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and disruptive spells.

This deck focuses on quickly and aggressively building up board presence and applying pressure to the opponent through efficient creatures and spells. By utilizing powerful cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, along with support from Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Galvanic Discharge, this Jeskai Aggro deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and finish the game quickly. The deck also includes disruptive elements such as Teferi, Time Raveler and Consign to Memory to control the tempo of the game. The mana base is constructed to ensure consistent and smooth mana curve for the aggressive strategy.

This deck focuses on generating mana quickly with cards like Pyretic Ritual and Desperate Ritual, while using cards like Manamorphose to dig through the deck and find combo pieces like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Grapeshot. By utilizing cards like Ruby Medallion and Artist's Talent, the deck can generate enough storm count to cast Past in Flames to flashback key spells from the graveyard. The goal is to assemble the combo pieces and cast a lethal Grapeshot to finish off the opponent in one explosive turn.

The main focus of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's hand and board while keeping control of the game. By using cards like Thoughtseize, Inquisition of Kozilek, and Smallpox, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and limit their resources. Additionally, The Rack and Raven's Crime put pressure on the opponent's hand size, while cards like Liliana of the Veil and Bontu's Last Reckoning provide additional control options. By utilizing these disruption tactics and maintaining a strong board presence with cards like Dauthi Voidwalker and Mishra's Factory, the deck aims to outlast and outplay the opponent.

This Mardu Aggro deck focuses on utilizing a mix of aggressive creatures, removal spells, and versatile planeswalkers to overwhelm the opponent. Cards like Guide of Souls and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer provide early pressure, while Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer help maintain board presence. The deck also includes disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the opponent's game plan. With a combination of efficient threats and disruptive elements, this deck aims to put the opponent on the back foot and close out the game quickly.

The deck focuses on disrupting and controlling the opponent's game plan through a combination of hand disruption, counterspells, and removal spells. It also aims to generate card advantage and tempo with cards like Preordain and Subtlety. The strategy revolves around surviving the early game, stabilizing the board with cards like Fatal Push and Murktide Regent, and eventually out-valuing the opponent with powerful finishers like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. The mana base is designed to consistently cast the deck's spells and ensure a smooth curve throughout the game.

This deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with creatures and then bringing them back into play using reanimation spells. It utilizes cards like Stitcher's Supplier and Eyetwitch to dump creatures into the graveyard, while spells like Persist and Archon of Cruelty allow for powerful creatures to be brought back into play. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with big, hard-hitting creatures that can quickly end the game. Additionally, cards like Witherbloom Charm and Thoughtseize help to disrupt the opponent's game plan and control the board.

Utilizing a combination of reanimation spells and powerful creatures, this deck aims to quickly cheat big threats like Griselbrand or Ulamog into play from the graveyard. The key strategy is to discard these creatures with cards like Faithless Looting and then reanimate them with cards like Goryo's Vengeance. The deck also includes disruption in the form of hand disruption and removal spells to control the game until it can assemble its combo. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats and win the game before they can recover.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by ramping up mana quickly with Eldrazi Temple and other mana sources to play powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul and Ulamog. It also utilizes cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Kozilek's Command to search for key pieces and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with massive creatures and powerful spells while keeping them in check through various forms of control. It seeks to establish dominance on the battlefield and dictate the pace of the game.

This deck focuses on playing aggressively with a mix of creatures and spells. It utilizes Leyline cards and other efficient spells to control the board while quickly dealing damage to the opponent. The deck features a range of different colored lands to support its diverse mana requirements. The creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Scion of Draco provide strong aggression, while spells like Stubborn Denial and Fatal Push help to protect and clear the way for them. The overall strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and powerful plays.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Broodscale Bloodchiefs and various support cards to quickly assemble a powerful combo that overwhelms the opponent. By using cards like Sowing Mycospawn and Blade of the Bloodchief to generate tokens and pump them up, along with ramping into Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End, the deck aims to create a dominating board state and finish off the opponent with big swings. The inclusion of control elements like Malevolent Rumble and Unholy Heat also help to disrupt the opponent's strategy and secure the win.
